Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 091. Arranging for Reserve Troops (ABL 0094)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL EN-ía

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Ṭab-ṣill-Ešarra. Good health to the king, my lord! May Aššur and Mullissu bless the king, my lord!

o 22

ARAD-ka mDÙG.GA—ṣil—É.ŠÁR.RA

o 33

lu DI-mu a-na LUGAL EN-ía

o 44

aš-šur d⸢NIN.LÍL⸣ a-na LUGAL

o 55

EN-ia lik-ru-bu

o 66

LUGAL be-li* i-sa-ap-ra

(6) The king, my lord, wrote to me: "Set out on the 22nd and be in the Inner City on the 29th!" — before I go to the Inner City, let me come and see the king, my lord!

o 77

ma-a UD 22-KÁM na*-me*-ši

o 88

ma-a UD 29-KÁM ina URU.⸢ŠÀ—URU⸣

o 99

lu-u at-ta [o]

o 1010

a-du i-na URU.ŠÀ—⸢URU⸣

o 1111

la al-lak-u-ni

o 1212

la-li*-ka* LUGAL be*-li

o 1313

la-mur ù ina UGU

(13) And as to the reserves of the king's men of the Ruqahu and Hallatu tribes about which the king my lord wrote to me, I sent (orders about them) on the very day I saw the (king's) letter.

o 1414

ša ku-tal ERIM—MAN

Reverse
r 1r 1

ša KUR.ru-qa-ḫa-a-a

r 22

ša KUR.ḫal-lat-a-a

r 33

ša LUGAL EN iš-pur-a-ni

r 44

UD-mu šá e-gír-tú

r 55

a-mur-u-ni a-sap-[ra]

rest uninscribed

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334043/.
